items.title: NT enacts 'significant overhaul' of child protection system

children.content: The Northern Territory parliament has passed legislative changes to the child protection system, with the Country Liberal Party (CLP) describing its new laws as "the most significant overhaul of the Territory's child protection system in a generation".

articles.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby child protection review finds departmental failings

children.content: Nearly three months since the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby, an interim report into her family circumstances prior to the tragedy has been handed down.

image.altText: A close up photo of a woman wearing a police uniform

articles.title: Protest held ahead of NT child protection laws debate

children.content: The Northern Territory parliament is debating controversial child protection laws that have been criticised for their potential impact on the Aboriginal Child Placement Principle.

image.altText: A large crowd of protesters standing on a grassed area, holding signs.

gallery.altText: Three Aboriginal boys seen from behind.

thumbnail.altText: Three Aboriginal boys seen from behind.

items.title: Police confirm investigation involving incident with NT administrator

children.content: Former army commando turned Labor MP, Luke Gosling, has denied involvement in an alleged incident with King Charles's representative in the NT.

image.altText: A man in a shirt and jacket behind a microphone

items.title: PM backs bid for future NT AFL team, no promises on funding

children.content: Anthony Albanese says he would love to see the creation of a Northern Territory AFL team while visiting the Tiwi Islands in the NT.

image.altText: The Tiwi Islands footy team with Anthony Albanese.

items.title: Weatherald retains Australian Test spot despite middling Ashes returns

children.content: Jake Weatherald is retained in Australia's 13-player Test squad for the upcoming series against Bangladesh.

image.altText: Australia batter Jake Weatherald plays a cover drive while down on one knee during a Test.

items.title: PM dashes hopes for new NT hospital during $10m maternity announcement

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese announces $10 million to improve maternity services at Royal Darwin Hospital, but maternity care advocates say the money is unlikely to improve staffing issues.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese at a press conference

items.title: Hundreds march on Pine Gap in secretive site's biggest protest in decades

children.content: About 300 people have marched on Pine Gap in Alice Springs, protesting against its role in US military operations, in the largest protest at the top-secret defence site since 1987.

articles.title: Hundreds gather in Alice Springs ahead of Pine Gap protest

children.content: Hundreds of activists and academics have gathered in Alice Springs to join Arrernte traditional owners ahead of a planned protest against the joint Australian-US military base at Pine Gap.

image.altText: Two older women smile for the camera.

gallery.altText: A crowd of protesters with police watching them.
A large group of  protesters gathered on a wide road, on one side of a roadblock.
A large group of protesters walking down a wide road, holding signs and flags.

thumbnail.altText: A crowd of protesters with police watching them.
A large group of  protesters gathered on a wide road, on one side of a roadblock.
A large group of protesters walking down a wide road, holding signs and flags.

items.title: 'It's insane': NT government divides opinion with plan to reduce block sizes

children.content: The Northern Territory government is planning to reduce the minimum block size in low-density suburbs from 800 to 450 square metres, sparking concern from some residents.

image.altText: A housing subdivision with half of the section built

items.title: Electric Tiwi football final sees back-to-back victory for Imalu Tigers

children.content: The Imalu Tigers and Tapalinga Super Stars are the new premiers of Tiwi Islands football, claiming victory in the men's and women's competitions in electric grand final matches on Sunday.

image.altText: A child in a yellow short on the shoulders of a man in a football jersey holding a red football with people gathered around them

items.title: Family's cross-contamination nightmare after contractors spread mould

children.content: A Queensland family is living in an overcrowded caravan and shed after insurance provider Suncorp exacerbated damage in their home, initially caused by a leak.

gallery.altText: Family of seven look at camera sitting inside their garage.
Inside a family home in Queensland, that has been damaged by insurer.

thumbnail.altText: Family of seven look at camera sitting inside their garage.
Inside a family home in Queensland, that has been damaged by insurer.

items.title: Woman's family wrongly told she was dead in hospital identity error

children.content: A Melbourne hospital apologises after mixing up the medical data of two people, resulting in one of them being wrongly declared dead by police.

image.altText: Michelle Buckley stands indoors, dressed in a warm cardigan.

items.title: Map reveals Australia's 'pharmacy deserts' are not all in the bush

children.content: Australia's "pharmacy deserts" have been mapped for the first time, revealing remote NT communities and outer-city Melbourne have fewer pharmacies per person than some other parts of the country.

image.altText: A female pharmacist is selecting a drug from the pharmacy inventory.

items.title: Gas tax, gambling and AI at stake at the Labor conference

children.content: Labor members and unions are gearing up for fights over gambling, gas and AI at the party's national conference later this week.

image.altText: A man in a suit and spectacles raises his arms at a podium at parliament.

items.title: How school is different for Jewish Australian kids

children.content: The Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion hears from Jewish school leaders about how they need to implement extraordinary and expensive security measures.

image.altText: Rows of empty black desks, each with a white booklet on top and either brown or cream seats in a green room.

items.title: Hegseth outlines cost of Iran war at Senate hearing

children.content: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth faces questioning on the cost of the war against Iran, his idea of testosterone screening for troops, and the number of service people who have died.

articles.title: 'Not finished': Trump signals US strikes on Iran's Pickaxe Mountain site

children.content: Donald Trump says the United States is far from finished with Iran, warning its next military target could be an underground nuclear complex.

image.altText: Donald Trump speaking while seated in the White House

gallery.altText: Pete Hegseth seated, speaking into a small microphone.

thumbnail.altText: Pete Hegseth seated, speaking into a small microphone.

items.title: Zelenskyy names new Ukrainian commander-in-chief

children.content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy replaces the country's commander-in-chief after protests against his sacking of the defence minister last week.

image.altText: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy speaks behind a lectern

items.title: Spanish chief accuses FIFA boss of 'destroying the football industry'

children.content: FIFA president Gianni Infantino comes in for more heavy criticism, this time at the hands of La Liga boss Javier Tebas.

image.altText: Gianni Infantino holds the World Cup trophy with Donald Trump

items.title: New Ozploitation horror is like Puberty Blues from hell

children.content: After spending years in special effects and make-up, filmmaker Mia'Kate Russell's ocker nightmare Penny Lane Is Dead has made it to the silver screen.

children.content: Where are macadamia nuts indigenous to? And what’s the capital city of Switzerland?

items.title: Stingers hitting new heights thanks to shared love for each other

children.content: Australia's women's water polo team, the Stingers, were the most successful Australian team at the Paris Olympics, winning a stunning silver medal. This is the secret to their success.

image.altText: Bronte Halligan, Sienna Green and Abby Andrews hug

items.title: Cameron McEvoy has lived a life between trips to Glasgow

children.content: As one of just two Australian swimmers returning to Glasgow for a second Commonwealth Games, Cameron McEvoy is taking time to appreciate how much has happened to him in the past 12 years.

gallery.altText: Cameron McEvoy at the Commonwealth Games and Pan Pacs swimming trials.

thumbnail.altText: Cameron McEvoy at the Commonwealth Games and Pan Pacs swimming trials.

items.title: AI companies 'stole' Australian culture, news bosses say, as they call for licensing deals

image.altText: Hamish MacDonald,  Allesandra Galloni, Hugh Marks, Michael Miller, Jane Palfreyman, Matt Stanton.

children.content: The heads of the ABC, SBS, Nine and News Corp say AI companies should strike licensing deals rather than freely train on Australian news, warning journalism is a public good that must be protected.

items.title: ASX rises as oil prices hit five-week high, Wall Street tech stocks rebound

children.content: The Australian share market has started its day higher after Wall Street's beaten-down tech and semiconductor stocks rebounded sharply overnight. Oil prices surged to a five-week high as Donald Trump signalled the US is "not finished" attacking Iran. Follow live.

image.altText: ASX board with stock names and prices blurred as board moves

items.title: Judgment day for Rebel Wilson's messy defamation case

children.content: Hollywood star Rebel Wilson will learn today whether she defamed the lead actor on her "fun girl-power" film, in a judgment to be handed down in the Federal Court.

image.altText: Rebel Wilson outside court wearing a green jacket.

items.title: Repatriation effort for homeless man found dead outside Sydney station

children.content: The mother of a homeless man whose death outside a Sydney train station went unnoticed for days is pleading for her son's remains to be returned to Nepal.

gallery.altText: Composite of Bikram and his mother Seti

thumbnail.altText: Composite of Bikram and his mother Seti

items.title: WA man dies after not being able to get antipsychotic medication

children.content: Bradley Buswell, aged in his 40s and living with schizophrenia, died in state care after his medication became unavailable due to national shortages. An ongoing coronial inquest is probing the circumstances of his death.

image.altText: Bradley Buswell smiles wearing a collared shirt.

items.title: What you need to know about Thursday's teacher strike in Victoria

children.content: Victorian state school teachers and educators are going ahead with their second 24-hour strike this year after union members rejected the government's latest pay offer.

image.altText: A dense crowd of people in red shirts with some holding signs walk through a CBD street.

items.title: Players and fans made the World Cup a success, but the politics shouldn't be forgotten

children.content: The biggest ever World Cup brought diverse communities together and galvanised its non-traditional footballing co-hosts, but the concerns raised from the start have not disappeared in the positive afterglow.

items.title: US rider hit by car in road rage incident before Tour de France time trial

children.content: Brandon McNulty is confronted by an angry driver on his way to the time trial as Remco Evenepoel shaves some time off Tadej Pogačar's overall lead.

image.altText: Brandon McNulty rides past a German flag at the Tour de France

items.title: 'Irresponsible': Russia conducts live-fire weapons exercise off British coast

children.content: A UK minister has confirmed that a Russian naval vessel conducted a "live-fire weapons exercise" in international waters off the coast of Plymouth in south-west England.

items.title: Iran war delays upgrades to Queensland’s Triple Zero phone system

children.content: The Iran war has been a contributing factor to Queensland's Triple Zero phone system being delayed until next year, the ABC understands.

items.title: Falling 200kg drive gear injures steel worker months after workplace death

children.content: There have been six incidents involving falling tools and building materials at BlueScope, less than a year since contractor Jack McGrath was killed.

image.altText: Worker looking out on industrial plant.

items.title: UK politician Ann Widdecombe hit 21 times with hammer, prosecutors allege

children.content: Joshua Kerry, 28, appears in the Westminster Magistrates' Court charged with murder over a killing that has shocked the public and political establishment.

image.altText: A politician speaks on stage.

items.title: Whyalla workers injured at steelworks' blast furnace

children.content: Three Whyalla steelworks employees have been treated for injuries suffered during attempts to restart the site's troubled blast furnace, the ABC understands.
